page: Microsoft Azure D48_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 50 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1229 GB of local storage. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% of tested servers. However, the high core count compensates in parallelized tasks, with multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and software compilation benchmarks consistently scoring in the middle 50% average tier. Cryptographic and database operations, along with Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, also perform at average levels. Large language model inference is limited, showing average prompt processing speeds but poor text generation throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48_v3 offers a high density of legacy vCPUs and memory, making it a viable option for workloads that benefit from parallel execution rather than raw single-threaded speed. The combination of 192.0 GB of RAM and 1229 GB of local storage makes this server suitable for multi-threaded applications, relational databases, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that do not require modern vector or matrix acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D48_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. While single-core CPU benchmarks place this instance in the bottom 10%, its multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations reside in the average middle 50% tier. This resource profile offers a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 1:4, making it qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized legacy workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded processing, static web serving, and medium-scale database deployments that do not require GPU acceleration or advanced AVX-512 instruction sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48_v3 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage. It delivers average multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, suited for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high core density and local storage for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
192.0 GB of system memory and 1229 GB of local storage
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Poor single-core performance and limited LLM text generation speeds
Well-suited for multi-threaded applications, databases, and web serving
